sections.heading: Syntax
sections.body:
| Feature                     | Supported |
| --------------------------- | --------- |
| arrow function expressions  | Yes       |
| classes                     | Yes       |
| super                       | Yes       |
| generators                  | Yes       |
| default function parameters | Yes       |
| rest parameters             | Yes       |
| spread iterables            | Yes       |
| object literal extensions   | Yes       |
| for...of loops              | Yes       |
| for await...of loops        | Yes       |
| octal and binary literals   | Yes       |
| template literals           | Yes       |
| destructuring assignment    | Yes       |
| new\.target                 | Yes       |
| RegExp -y and -u flags      | No        |
| Exponentiation (\*\*)       | Yes       |

sections.heading: Built-In Objects
sections.body:
| Feature    | Supported |
| ---------- | --------- |
| BigInt     | No        |
| Map        | Yes       |
| Promise    | Yes       |
| Proxy      | No        |
| Reflect    | No        |
| Set        | Yes       |
| Symbol     | Yes       |
| TypedArray | Yes       |
| WeakMap    | Yes       |
| WeakSet    | No        |

sections.heading: Built-In Methods & Properties
sections.body:
| Feature                     | Supported |
| --------------------------- | --------- |
| Object static methods       | Yes       |
| String static methods       | Yes       |
| String.prototype methods    | Yes       |
| RegExp.prototype properties | No        |
| Function.name               | Yes       |
| Array static methods        | No        |
| Array.prototype methods     | Yes       |
| Number static methods       | No        |
| Math methods                | No        |

sections.heading: Built-In Modules
sections.body:
You can [import and use](atlas-import-external-dependencies) standard
Node built-in modules in [Atlas Function](atlas-functions). Atlas Functions
support most built-ins with either full or partial support. Some
built-ins that are not suited for serverless workloads are not
supported.

> **Note:** The supported modules and partially supported modules are compatible
> with [Node API version 10.18.1](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/). Avoid
> using APIs in these modules introduced after or deprecated since Node
> 10.18.1.

sections.heading: Fully Supported Modules
sections.body:
Atlas Functions fully support the following built-in modules:

- [assert](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/assert.html)

- [buffer](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/buffer.html)

- [events](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/events.html)

- [net](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/net.html)

- [os](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/os.html)

- [path](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/path.html)

- [punycode](https://github.com/bestiejs/punycode.js)

  > **Note:** The [built-in punycode module is deprecated](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/punycode.html). However, Atlas Functions
  > provide the [punycode](https://www.npmjs.com/package/punycode) module from `npm`
  > automatically. You can import the module with:
  >
  > ```javascript
  > const punycode = require("punycode");
  > ```

- [querystring](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/querystring.html)

- [stream](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/stream.html)

- [string\_decoder](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/string_decoder.html)

- [timers](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/timers.html)

- [tls](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/tls.html)

- [tty](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/tty.html)

- [url](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/url.html)

- [zlib](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/zlib.html)

sections.heading: Partially Supported Modules
sections.body: Atlas Functions support a subset of the functionality of the following modules.

sections.heading: dgram
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support the following `dgram` APIs:

- [socket.addMembership](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_addmembership_multicastaddress_multicastinterface)

- [socket.address](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_address)

- [socket.bind](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_bind_port_address_callback)

- [socket.close](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_close_callback)

- [createSocket](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_dgram_createsocket_options_callback)

- [socket.dropMembership](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_dropmembership_multicastaddress_multicastinterface)

- [socket.send](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_send_msg_offset_length_port_address_callback)

- [socket.setBroadcast](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_setbroadcast_flag)

- [socket.setMulticastLoopback](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_setmulticastloopback_flag)

- [socket.setMulticastTTL](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_setmulticastttl_ttl)

Atlas Functions do **not** support the following `dgram` APIs:

- [socket.ref](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_ref)

- [socket.setTTL](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_setttl_ttl)

- [socket.unref](https://nodejs.org/api/dgram.html#dgram_socket_unref)

sections.heading: dns
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support the [dns](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/dns.html) module with the following **exceptions**:

- Atlas Functions do **not** support the [dns Promises API](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/dns.html#dns_dns_promises_api)

- Atlas Functions do **not** support [resolver.cancel()](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/dns.html#dns_resolver_cancel)

sections.heading: fs
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support the following `fs` APIs:

- [accessSync](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/fs.html#fs_fs_accesssync_path_mode)

- [constants](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/fs.html#fs_fs_constants)

- [lstatSync](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/fs.html#fs_fs_lstatsync_path_options)

- [readFileSync](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/fs.html#fs_fs_readfilesync_path_options)

- [statSync](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/fs.html#fs_fs_statsync_path_options)

sections.heading: http, http/2 and https
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support all of the [http](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/http.html)
and [https](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/https.html) APIs **except**
for the [Server](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/http.html#http_class_http_server)
class functionality.

Similarly, Atlas Functions support only the client-side APIs of
[http/2](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/http2.html).

> **Note:** 
>
> axios Supported for Sending HTTP Requests
>
> Atlas Functions support v1.3.6 of the HTTP library,
> [axios](https://www.npmjs.com/package/axios/v/1.3.6). You can replace
> HTTP requests sent through an [HTTP Service](atlas/app-services/services/http)
> client with calls to an HTTP library like axios.

sections.heading: process
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support the following `process` APIs:

- [hrTime](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/process.html#process_process_hrtime_time)

- [nextTick](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/process.html#process_process_nexttick_callback_args)

- [version](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/process.html#process_process_version)

- [versions](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/process.html#process_process_versions)

sections.heading: util
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support the [util](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/util.html) module with the following **exceptions**:

- Atlas Functions do **not** support [util.TextEncoder](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/util.html#util_class_util_textencoder)

- Atlas Functions do **not** support [util.TextDecoder](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/util.html#util_class_util_textdecoder)

sections.heading: crypto
sections.body:
Atlas Functions support the [crypto](https://nodejs.org/api/crypto.html) module with
the following **exceptions**:

- Atlas Functions do **not** support [crypto.createDiffieHellman()](https://nodejs.org/api/crypto.html#crypto_crypto_creatediffiehellman_prime_primeencoding_generator_generatorencoding)

- Atlas Functions do **not** support [crypto.createDiffieHellmanGroup()](https://nodejs.org/api/crypto.html#crypto_crypto_creatediffiehellmangroup_name)

- Atlas Functions do **not** support [crypto.createECDH()](https://nodejs.org/api/crypto.html#crypto_crypto_createecdh_curvename)

sections.heading: Unsupported Modules
sections.body:
Atlas Functions **do not** support the following built-in
modules:

- `child_process`

- `cluster`

- `domain`

- `readline`

- `v8`

- `vm`
